title: Data Analyst
description: Data Analysts collect, process, and analyze datasets to help organizations make informed decisions. They transform raw numbers into actionable business insights using statistical methods and visualization tools.
dayInLife: A typical day involves cleaning datasets in Python, building dashboards in Tableau, and presenting findings to stakeholders in weekly review meetings.
notSuitedFor: People who dislike repetitive data cleaning
Those who prefer purely creative work
beginnerProjects: Analyze a public Kaggle dataset
Build a sales dashboard in Excel
Create a COVID data visualization

title: UX Researcher
description: UX Researchers study how users think, feel, and behave to inform product design decisions. They conduct interviews, usability tests, and surveys to uncover insights that make products more intuitive and valuable.
dayInLife: Your day might include moderating user interviews, synthesizing findings into insight reports, and presenting user pain points to product and design teams.
notSuitedFor: People who dislike ambiguity
Those who prefer building over observing
requiredEducation: Psychology, Sociology, HCI, or Design preferred
entryPathways: Junior researcher at a design agency
Product research role at a startup
beginnerProjects: Interview 5 users about a daily app
Run a usability test on a local website
Create an empathy map for a product you use
portfolioIdeas: Case studies on Notion or Medium

title: Product Manager
description: Product Managers own the vision, strategy, and roadmap of a product. They sit at the intersection of business, technology, and user experience, aligning cross-functional teams to build products people love.
dayInLife: You'll run sprint planning with engineers, review analytics dashboards, interview customers, and write PRDs — all in the same day.
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer deep technical work
People who dislike managing stakeholder expectations
entryPathways: Internal transition from engineering or design
beginnerProjects: Write a PRD for an app you wish existed
Create a product teardown of a popular app
Build a simple no-code product and collect user feedback

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Cybersecurity Analysts protect an organization's digital assets from threats and attacks. They monitor networks, investigate incidents, and implement security controls to keep systems safe.
dayInLife: You review security alerts, investigate suspicious activity, patch vulnerabilities, and document incidents while staying updated on the latest threat intelligence.
suitedFor: Those with patience for repetitive monitoring
notSuitedFor: People who prefer creative freedom
Those uncomfortable with high-stakes responsibility
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in CS, IT, or related field
beginnerProjects: Set up a home lab with VirtualBox
Complete TryHackMe beginner rooms
Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions

title: Ethical Hacker
description: Ethical Hackers (Penetration Testers) are hired to legally break into systems to find vulnerabilities before malicious hackers do. It's one of the most exciting and fast-growing roles in tech.
dayInLife: You spend your day probing web applications for SQL injection flaws, testing network defenses, writing detailed vulnerability reports, and briefing clients on findings.
notSuitedFor: People who need rigid structure
Those uncomfortable with ambiguous legal/ethical lines
requiredEducation: CS degree helpful but not required
Certifications often more valued than degrees
beginnerProjects: Complete OWASP WebGoat
Participate in HackerOne public programs
Set up Metasploitable lab

title: Technical Writer
description: Technical Writers create clear, concise documentation for software, APIs, products, and processes. They bridge the gap between complex technical information and users who need to understand it.
dayInLife: You interview engineers to understand a new API, write developer docs, review product changelogs, and create user guides — all requiring both technical understanding and writing craft.
suitedFor: People who enjoy explaining complex things simply
notSuitedFor: People who dislike research and fact-checking
Those who prefer coding over writing
requiredEducation: English, Communications, CS, or Engineering preferred
entryPathways: Content writer to tech writer transition
beginnerProjects: Document an open-source project
Write API docs for a public API
Create a user guide for an app you use
portfolioIdeas: Technical writing portfolio on GitHub Pages

title: UI Designer
description: UI Designers craft the visual interfaces of digital products — every button, icon, screen, and interaction. They combine aesthetics with usability to create visually stunning, functional designs.
dayInLife: You create high-fidelity mockups in Figma, build component libraries, collaborate with developers for pixel-perfect implementation, and iterate based on feedback.
suitedFor: Those with strong attention to detail
People who love aesthetics and functionality together
notSuitedFor: People who dislike feedback on their creative work
Those who prefer purely technical roles
requiredEducation: Design, Fine Arts, or CS preferred
entryPathways: Junior designer at agency or startup
beginnerProjects: Redesign a poorly designed app
Create a UI kit in Figma
Participate in Daily UI challenge

title: Graphic Designer
description: Graphic Designers create visual content for brands, marketing materials, social media, packaging, and print. They use imagery, typography, and color to communicate ideas and build brand identity.
dayInLife: You design social media creatives, create brand guidelines, work on print materials, and collaborate with marketing teams to bring campaigns to life.
notSuitedFor: People who dislike subjective client feedback
Those who prefer highly structured technical roles
requiredEducation: Fine Arts, Graphic Design, or Visual Communication preferred
beginnerProjects: Design a logo for a local business
Create social media templates
Redesign a brand identity

title: Content Strategist
description: Content Strategists plan, develop, and manage content across channels to meet business goals. They map the content ecosystem, align messaging to audience needs, and drive organic growth through strategic storytelling.
dayInLife: You audit existing content, develop a 3-month content calendar, brief writers and designers, and analyze performance metrics to refine the strategy.
suitedFor: Strategic thinkers with a writing background
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer purely creative writing without strategy
People who dislike data-driven decisions
requiredEducation: English, Marketing, Journalism, or Communications preferred
beginnerProjects: Create a content strategy for a personal blog
Audit and improve a small brand's content
Build a 3-month editorial calendar

title: Digital Marketing Strategist
description: Digital Marketing Strategists plan and execute campaigns across search, social, email, and paid channels to grow brands online. They combine data analysis with creative thinking to drive measurable results.
dayInLife: You review campaign performance dashboards, brief the design team on creatives, optimize ad spend, and present monthly growth metrics to leadership.
notSuitedFor: People who dislike constant performance pressure
Those who prefer purely creative work
beginnerProjects: Run a ₹500 Google Ads campaign
Grow a social media account from 0
Build an email sequence and track open rates
portfolioIdeas: Campaign case studies with real metrics

title: SEO Specialist
description: SEO Specialists improve a website's visibility in search engine results through on-page optimization, link building, and technical fixes. They turn organic search into a sustainable growth channel.
dayInLife: You audit a site for technical issues, research keyword opportunities, brief writers on optimized content, and track ranking changes in weekly reports.
notSuitedFor: Those who need immediate results
People who dislike technical troubleshooting
beginnerProjects: Optimize a personal blog for 10 keywords
Fix technical SEO issues on a practice site
Build 10 backlinks in 30 days
portfolioIdeas: Case studies on client ranking improvements

title: Performance Marketer
description: Performance Marketers run paid advertising campaigns on Google, Meta, and other platforms with a focus on measurable ROI. They optimize cost per acquisition, ROAS, and conversion funnels.
dayInLife: You monitor ad spend hourly, A/B test creatives, adjust bidding strategies, and build attribution reports to show exactly which campaigns are driving revenue.
suitedFor: People who love numbers and optimization
Those comfortable with spending large budgets responsibly
notSuitedFor: Creative-only thinkers
People who prefer brand building over direct response
beginnerProjects: Run a Google Search campaign with ₹500
Build a Facebook lookalike audience
Set up full conversion tracking for a site

title: Financial Analyst
description: Financial Analysts evaluate financial data, build models, and provide recommendations to help businesses and investors make sound financial decisions.
dayInLife: You build DCF models in Excel, analyze quarterly earnings reports, prepare investment memos, and present findings to senior management or clients.
suitedFor: People who enjoy research and modeling
notSuitedFor: People who dislike high-pressure deadlines
Those who prefer creative or people-facing work
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Finance, Commerce, or Economics
MBA or CFA is a strong advantage
beginnerProjects: Build a DCF model for a listed company
Analyze quarterly earnings of a stock
Create a personal portfolio tracker

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description: Investment Banking Analysts help companies raise capital, execute mergers and acquisitions, and advise on complex financial transactions. It's one of the most demanding and rewarding entry-level finance roles.
dayInLife: You build pitch decks and financial models until late nights, attend client calls, run due diligence on acquisition targets, and coordinate with lawyers and accountants.
suitedFor: Those seeking maximum income and exposure
notSuitedFor: People who value work-life balance highly
Those uncomfortable with 80+ hour weeks
requiredEducation: Top-tier university is a strong advantage
beginnerProjects: Build a full LBO model
Create an M&A synergy analysis
Write an equity research report

freeResources.title: Breaking Into Wall Street Free Course

title: Business Analyst
description: Business Analysts bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions. They gather requirements, document processes, and ensure technology projects deliver real business value.
dayInLife: You facilitate requirement workshops, write user stories, create process flow diagrams, and serve as liaison between stakeholders and engineering teams.
suitedFor: Problem solvers comfortable with both business and technology
notSuitedFor: People who prefer deep technical coding
Those who dislike documentation
beginnerProjects: Document the business process of a local shop
Write user stories for a mobile app
Create a SWOT analysis for a startup

title: AI Automation Specialist
description: AI Automation Specialists design and deploy AI-powered workflows that automate repetitive business processes. Using tools like Zapier, Make, and custom LLM integrations, they help teams do more with less.
dayInLife: You map a client's manual process, build a Zapier workflow connected to an AI model, test it, and hand over a fully automated pipeline that saves hours of manual work.
notSuitedFor: People who prefer deep research over implementation
Those who dislike constant tool updates
preferredBackgrounds: Any field with operational or business experience
beginnerProjects: Automate your email to Notion workflow
Build a chatbot with no-code tools
Create a lead gen automation for a local business

title: Prompt Engineer
description: Prompt Engineers design, test, and refine input prompts for large language models to get reliable, accurate, and useful outputs. They sit at the intersection of linguistics, psychology, and AI.
dayInLife: You experiment with prompt variations, evaluate model outputs, document effective patterns, and build evaluation frameworks to measure AI response quality.
notSuitedFor: People who want to build traditional software products
Those who dislike ambiguous outputs
requiredEducation: Linguistics, CS, or Cognitive Science helpful
entryPathways: Internal AI team at large enterprise
beginnerProjects: Build a customer support bot with effective prompts
Create a prompt library for a use case
Run a benchmark comparing prompt styles

title: No-Code Developer
description: No-Code Developers build functional web apps, databases, and automated workflows without writing traditional code. Using tools like Webflow, Bubble, and Glide, they deliver products at startup speed.
dayInLife: You design a client's web app in Bubble, connect it to Airtable for data, set up payment flows, and deploy a working MVP in a week instead of months.
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer deep software engineering
People who dislike visual drag-and-drop tools
beginnerProjects: Build a simple directory website in Webflow
Create a task manager in Bubble
Build a portfolio app with Glide

title: Community Manager
description: Community Managers build and nurture online communities around brands, products, or causes. They engage users, moderate discussions, run events, and turn passive followers into passionate advocates.
dayInLife: You reply to community questions on Discord, plan a monthly AMA session, create a weekly newsletter for members, and report engagement metrics to the marketing lead.
suitedFor: People who genuinely enjoy helping others
notSuitedFor: Introverts who dislike constant social interaction
Those who prefer working with data over people
beginnerProjects: Build and grow a Discord server to 100 members
Run a 30-day community challenge
Create an engagement playbook

title: Customer Success Manager
description: Customer Success Managers ensure that clients get maximum value from a product or service. They build relationships, drive adoption, reduce churn, and identify expansion opportunities.
dayInLife: You run a quarterly business review with an enterprise client, troubleshoot an onboarding issue for a new customer, and flag at-risk accounts to the sales team.
suitedFor: People who enjoy problem-solving for others
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer minimal client interaction
People who dislike repetitive relationship management
beginnerProjects: Create an onboarding checklist for a product
Map a customer journey
Write a QBR template

title: Product Marketing Associate
description: Product Marketing Associates position products in the market, craft messaging, create launch campaigns, and enable sales teams. They deeply understand both the product and the customer.
dayInLife: You interview customers to understand pain points, write a positioning document, create sales enablement materials, and coordinate a product launch campaign.
suitedFor: Bridge builders between marketing and product
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer performance metrics over narrative
People who dislike cross-team coordination
beginnerProjects: Write a positioning doc for a real product
Create a competitor analysis
Build a go-to-market plan for a hypothetical launch

title: Consumer Insights Analyst
description: Consumer Insights Analysts collect and interpret data about consumer behavior, preferences, and motivations to inform product and marketing strategy. They blend market research with data analysis.
dayInLife: You design and launch a survey, analyze focus group recordings, build a report on shifting consumer preferences, and present findings to the brand team.
suitedFor: Storytellers who can translate data into insight
notSuitedFor: People who dislike rigorous data collection
Those who prefer execution over research
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Marketing, Psychology, Economics, or Statistics
beginnerProjects: Conduct a 50-person survey and publish findings
Analyze public Nielsen data
Write a consumer trend report

title: Behavioral Designer
description: Behavioral Designers apply principles from psychology, behavioral economics, and persuasion to design products and communications that nudge people toward better decisions.
dayInLife: You study user drop-off patterns, apply behavioral frameworks like EAST or nudge theory, redesign an onboarding flow, and run A/B tests to measure behavior change.
suitedFor: Researchers who want to apply findings
notSuitedFor: Pure visual designers
People uncomfortable with ethical complexity
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Psychology, Behavioral Economics, Design, or related
beginnerProjects: Apply loss aversion to an email subject line
Redesign a sign-up form using behavioral principles
Write a behavioral audit of an app

title: Game Designer
description: Game Designers create the rules, mechanics, narratives, and systems that make games engaging. They work at the intersection of psychology, storytelling, and systems design.
dayInLife: You design a new level mechanic, playtest it with colleagues, adjust difficulty curves based on feedback, document the game design document (GDD), and collaborate with artists and programmers.
notSuitedFor: People who only enjoy playing but not dissecting games
Those who dislike iterative feedback loops
requiredEducation: Game Design, CS, Fine Arts, or self-taught
beginnerProjects: Build a game in Godot or GameMaker
Participate in a 48-hour game jam
Design a board game and playtest it

title: Game Economy Designer
description: Game Economy Designers build the in-game economic systems — currencies, rewards, monetization loops, and balance mechanics that keep players engaged and games financially sustainable.
dayInLife: You model virtual currency flows in spreadsheets, analyze player spending data, adjust loot table drop rates, and collaborate with monetization teams to improve revenue without harming fun.
suitedFor: Economics or math enthusiasts who love games
notSuitedFor: Pure creatives without analytical skills
People who find spreadsheets tedious
requiredEducation: Economics, Math, Statistics, or Game Design preferred
entryPathways: Junior game designer who focuses on systems
Academic economist moving to gaming industry
beginnerProjects: Model a virtual economy for an existing game
Analyze a free-to-play game's monetization
Design a reward system for a hypothetical game

title: Sports Data Analyst
description: Sports Data Analysts use statistical methods and data science to analyze athletic performance, optimize team strategy, and inform player recruitment. Popularized by Moneyball-style thinking.
dayInLife: You parse match tracking data, build performance dashboards for coaching staff, run regression models to predict injury risk, and present scouting reports for player acquisition.
suitedFor: Data analysts with a passion for sports
notSuitedFor: Pure athletes with no data interest
People who dislike working with large datasets
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Statistics, Mathematics, CS, or Sports Science
entryPathways: Data analyst who pivots to sports
Sports science graduate with coding skills
beginnerProjects: Analyze IPL data on Kaggle
Build an Expected Goals model for football
Create a cricket player comparison dashboard

title: Sustainability Consultant
description: Sustainability Consultants help organizations reduce their environmental footprint, meet ESG goals, and create genuine impact strategies. They bridge environmental science with business strategy.
dayInLife: You conduct a carbon footprint audit for a manufacturing client, develop a sustainability roadmap, benchmark against industry standards, and write an ESG impact report.
suitedFor: Environmentally motivated people with business acumen
notSuitedFor: People who want purely field-based environmental work
Those uncomfortable with corporate environments
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Environmental Science, Business, or Engineering
beginnerProjects: Calculate carbon footprint for a local business
Write an ESG report for a public company
Map supply chain sustainability risks

title: ESG Analyst
description: ESG Analysts evaluate companies on Environmental, Social, and Governance criteria to help investors make responsible investment decisions and help companies improve their sustainability practices.
dayInLife: You score companies on ESG metrics, research governance controversies, update ESG databases, and present findings to fund managers making investment decisions.
suitedFor: Finance professionals who care about impact
notSuitedFor: People who want immediate impact over analysis
Those who dislike spreadsheet-heavy research
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Finance, Economics, Environmental Science
entryPathways: Financial analyst who pivots to ESG
beginnerProjects: Build an ESG scorecard for 5 companies
Analyze a company's ESG report
Create a responsible investing framework

title: Climate Risk Analyst
description: Climate Risk Analysts assess how climate change creates financial and operational risk for businesses, investors, and governments. They work at the intersection of climate science and financial risk.
dayInLife: You model flood risk scenarios for a bank's loan portfolio, evaluate transition risk from carbon regulations, and prepare climate risk disclosures for TCFD frameworks.
suitedFor: Scientists with financial or policy interest
notSuitedFor: Those who want quick career paths
People who dislike complex modeling
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Environmental Science, Climate Science, Finance, or Engineering
entryPathways: Climate science graduate moving to finance
beginnerProjects: Assess physical climate risk for a real estate portfolio
Build a carbon transition risk model
Write a TCFD-aligned disclosure

title: Instructional Designer
description: Instructional Designers create structured learning experiences for corporate training, e-learning, and educational platforms. They apply learning science to make complex content engaging and effective.
dayInLife: You storyboard a 30-minute e-learning module, build it in Articulate 360, review voiceover scripts, and test learning outcomes with a pilot group.
suitedFor: Educators who want to work in corporate or tech
People who love simplifying complex topics
notSuitedFor: People who dislike systematic documentation
Those who want fast-paced environments
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Education, Psychology, English, or any field
Instructional Design certification is highly valued
beginnerProjects: Build a 10-slide e-learning course in Rise 360
Create a learning needs analysis for a skill
Design a quiz-based assessment

title: HR Analyst
description: HR Analysts use data to improve hiring, retention, workforce planning, and employee experience. They turn people data into actionable insights that drive organizational decisions.
dayInLife: You analyze attrition data, build a headcount forecast model, measure diversity metrics, and present HR dashboard insights to leadership.
notSuitedFor: Pure creative thinkers
People who dislike data-heavy work
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in HR, Psychology, Statistics, or Business
beginnerProjects: Build an employee attrition model
Create a diversity dashboard
Analyze exit interview data

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist
description: Talent Acquisition Specialists find, attract, and hire top talent for organizations. They manage the full recruitment lifecycle from job description to offer letter.
dayInLife: You post job descriptions, screen 30 resumes, conduct phone screens, coordinate technical interviews, and negotiate offers for three open positions.
suitedFor: People who enjoy connecting individuals to opportunities
notSuitedFor: People who dislike repetitive outreach
Those who prefer deep analytical work
beginnerProjects: Source 20 candidates for a mock role
Write a job description for 3 roles
Build a structured interview guide

title: Operations Manager
description: Operations Managers oversee the day-to-day functioning of business operations, ensuring processes run efficiently, resources are optimized, and teams deliver on goals.
dayInLife: You review daily KPIs, resolve a supply chain bottleneck, lead a process improvement workshop, and present quarterly operational performance to the leadership team.
notSuitedFor: Detail-avoiders
People who prefer creative over operational work
requiredEducation: Business, Engineering, or Operations Management preferred
beginnerProjects: Map and improve a process in your college or home
Create a weekly KPI dashboard
Run a cost-reduction analysis

title: Supply Chain Analyst
description: Supply Chain Analysts optimize the flow of goods, services, and information from suppliers to customers. They use data to improve inventory, procurement, logistics, and supplier performance.
dayInLife: You analyze inventory levels to prevent stockouts, model demand forecasts, evaluate supplier performance metrics, and present cost-saving opportunities to procurement leadership.
suitedFor: Those with business and tech interest
notSuitedFor: Purely creative individuals
Those who dislike technical spreadsheet work
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Supply Chain, Business, Engineering, or Statistics
beginnerProjects: Build a demand forecast model
Analyze a supply chain case study
Map a product's supply chain end-to-end

title: Logistics Coordinator
description: Logistics Coordinators manage the movement of goods, coordinate with carriers and suppliers, track shipments, and ensure timely delivery. They are the operational backbone of supply chains.
dayInLife: You track 50 active shipments, resolve a customs clearance issue, coordinate with three freight forwarders, and update the logistics dashboard for the operations manager.
notSuitedFor: People who dislike repetitive coordination
Those who prefer creative freedom
requiredEducation: Supply Chain, Business, or Commerce preferred
beginnerProjects: Simulate a shipment tracking system in Excel
Map a last-mile delivery process
Write an SOP for logistics documentation

title: Copywriter
description: Copywriters craft persuasive words that sell products, build brands, and drive action. From ad headlines to website copy to email campaigns — copywriters make ideas come alive through language.
dayInLife: You write three ad variations for an Instagram campaign, craft a landing page headline, revise a product description, and brainstorm taglines for a new brand.
suitedFor: People who enjoy persuasion and psychology
notSuitedFor: People who dislike deadlines and revisions
Those who prefer technical over creative work
beginnerProjects: Write 10 ad headlines for a product you use
Rewrite a bad landing page you find online
Create a brand voice guide for a fictional company
portfolioIdeas: Portfolio site with copy samples by channel

title: Scriptwriter
description: Scriptwriters create written content for films, TV shows, web series, YouTube videos, ads, and podcasts. They translate ideas into dialogue, action, and narrative structure.
dayInLife: You write a 10-minute YouTube script, get notes from the director, revise two scenes, brainstorm episode arcs, and pitch a series concept to a platform.
suitedFor: People who love film and media
Creative problem solvers with narrative instincts
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike subjective critique
People who need structured, predictable roles
requiredEducation: Mass Communication, Film, or English helpful
beginnerProjects: Write a 5-minute short film script
Script a YouTube video for a popular creator
Adapt a news story into a 3-scene drama

title: Journalist
description: Journalists investigate, research, and report stories that inform the public. From breaking news to long-form investigations, they serve as society's watchdogs and storytellers.
dayInLife: You follow up on three story leads, conduct an interview with a government official, write a 1,200-word investigative piece, and fact-check before the 6pm deadline.
suitedFor: Strong writers who work well under pressure
People motivated by truth and public interest
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike deadlines or confrontation
People who need high income stability
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Journalism, Mass Communication, or English
beginnerProjects: Publish 5 articles on a blog
Cover a local event for a college publication
Conduct and publish a profile interview
portfolioIdeas: Published articles portfolio (Muck Rack or personal site)

title: Motion Designer
description: Motion Designers bring graphics and illustrations to life through animation for video, UI, social media, and film. They combine design skills with animation principles to create compelling visual storytelling.
dayInLife: You animate an explainer video for a startup, create an Instagram story animation, apply motion principles to an app's microinteractions, and review final renders.
suitedFor: Visual creatives with patience for technical work
People who love animation and film
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike technical software learning curves
People who need fast turnarounds
requiredEducation: Design, Fine Arts, or Film preferred
beginnerProjects: Animate a logo reveal in After Effects
Create a 30-second product demo animation
Build an animated infographic

title: AR/VR Designer
description: AR/VR Designers create immersive experiences for augmented and virtual reality platforms. They design spatial interfaces, 3D environments, and interactive experiences for the next generation of computing.
dayInLife: You prototype a spatial UI in Unity, test a VR training module on a headset, iterate on hand-tracking interactions, and present an AR retail experience concept to a client.
notSuitedFor: 2D-only designers
People uncomfortable with rapid technological change
requiredEducation: Design, CS, or Game Design preferred
beginnerProjects: Build a basic VR room in Unity
Create an AR filter in Spark AR
Design a spatial UI prototype

title: Public Policy Analyst
description: Public Policy Analysts research and evaluate government policies, regulations, and social programs. They provide evidence-based recommendations to shape laws and public decisions.
dayInLife: You review a proposed healthcare regulation, conduct stakeholder interviews, write a policy brief, and present findings to a government ministry or think tank.
suitedFor: People passionate about governance and social systems
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike slow, bureaucratic processes
People who want quick commercial outcomes
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Public Policy, Political Science, Economics, or Law
beginnerProjects: Write a policy brief on a local issue
Analyze a government budget allocation
Evaluate a social program's effectiveness

title: Legal Operations Associate
description: Legal Operations Associates improve the efficiency, technology, and business management of legal departments. They work at the intersection of law, technology, and project management.
dayInLife: You optimize a contract review workflow using AI tools, manage legal spend dashboards, coordinate with outside counsel, and implement a new contract management system.
notSuitedFor: Those who want to practice courtroom law
People who dislike process optimization
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Law, Business, or Technology
LLB helpful but not required for operations roles
beginnerProjects: Map a contract review process
Evaluate 3 contract management tools
Build a legal spend tracker in Excel

title: Compliance Analyst
description: Compliance Analysts ensure that organizations follow laws, regulations, and internal policies. They assess risk, develop controls, conduct audits, and train staff on regulatory requirements.
dayInLife: You review new RBI guidelines, update compliance policies, conduct a department audit, and prepare a compliance report for the board.
notSuitedFor: Highly creative people
Those who dislike repetitive monitoring tasks
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Law, Finance, Business, or Commerce
beginnerProjects: Audit a fictional company against SEBI regulations
Create a GDPR compliance checklist
Write a risk assessment for a process

title: Forensic Accountant
description: Forensic Accountants investigate financial fraud, embezzlement, and disputes. They work with law enforcement, lawyers, and corporations to uncover financial irregularities through detailed analysis.
dayInLife: You analyze 5 years of financial transactions for signs of fraud, prepare an expert witness report, interview employees as part of an internal investigation, and present findings to legal counsel.
suitedFor: Investigators at heart with financial acumen
notSuitedFor: People who prefer building over investigating
Those who dislike confrontational environments
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Accounting, Finance, or Commerce
beginnerProjects: Analyze a published corporate fraud case
Build a fraud detection checklist
Study the Satyam or Enron case in depth

title: Social Impact Consultant
description: Social Impact Consultants help NGOs, social enterprises, and corporations design and measure programs that create genuine social and environmental change. They bridge business rigor with mission-driven work.
dayInLife: You design a theory of change for an education NGO, measure impact outcomes, prepare a donor-facing impact report, and advise on scaling a rural livelihood program.
suitedFor: People who want income and purpose together
Those who understand both corporate and social sectors
notSuitedFor: People who need immediate financial rewards
Those uncomfortable with ambiguous impact metrics
requiredEducation: Social Work, Public Policy, Business, or Development Studies preferred
beginnerProjects: Design a Theory of Change for a local problem
Volunteer and document a program's outcomes
Write an impact report for an NGO

title: NGO Program Manager
description: NGO Program Managers design, implement, and oversee social programs that address community needs. They manage budgets, teams, field operations, and donor relationships.
dayInLife: You review field team reports from a literacy program, address a budget variance, write a quarterly donor update, and prepare for a board meeting on program expansion.
suitedFor: People who thrive in field-to-desk environments
notSuitedFor: Those who need corporate-level salaries
People who dislike bureaucratic processes
requiredEducation: Social Work, Development Studies, or Management preferred
beginnerProjects: Volunteer and manage a community project
Write a program implementation plan
Design a monitoring framework

title: Teacher / Educator
description: Teachers design and deliver learning experiences that shape knowledge, values, and skills. From school classrooms to online platforms, educators work across every level of learning.
dayInLife: You prepare lesson plans, conduct two classes, mentor struggling students, grade assignments, and collaborate with colleagues on curriculum improvements.
suitedFor: Those motivated by long-term impact on others
notSuitedFor: People who need high income immediately
Those who dislike repetitive content delivery
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Education (B.Ed) or any subject
B.Ed or teaching license required for school roles
entryPathways: Online teaching on platforms like Vedantu or Unacademy
beginnerProjects: Teach a topic to 5 peers and get feedback
Create a YouTube educational video
Design a course module for a skill

title: Career Counselor
description: Career Counselors guide individuals in discovering suitable career paths, making educational choices, and planning professional development. They blend psychology, coaching, and career knowledge.
dayInLife: You conduct three one-on-one counseling sessions, review aptitude test results with a student, update your resource library, and deliver a group workshop on resume writing.
suitedFor: People who love connecting others to opportunities
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike emotional conversations
People who prefer purely analytical roles
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Psychology, Education, or Social Work
Master's in Counseling or Career Guidance preferred
beginnerProjects: Counsel 10 students for free and document outcomes
Create a career resource library
Develop a career assessment quiz

title: Sales Development Representative
description: Sales Development Representatives (SDRs) generate new business opportunities by prospecting, cold outreach, and qualifying leads for the sales team. It's the highest-leverage entry point into tech sales.
dayInLife: You send 50 personalized cold emails, make 20 LinkedIn connection requests, follow up on warm leads, book 3 demos for the account executive, and log activities in CRM.
suitedFor: People who thrive on rejection and persistence
notSuitedFor: Those who need stability before results
Introverts who dislike cold outreach
requiredEducation: Any Bachelor's degree or no degree
beginnerProjects: Write 10 personalized cold email sequences
Build a prospect list of 50 target companies
Practice 5 discovery call roleplays

title: Creator Partnerships Manager
description: Creator Partnerships Managers build and maintain relationships between brands and content creators (YouTubers, Instagram influencers, podcasters). They negotiate deals, manage campaigns, and measure ROI.
dayInLife: You reach out to 10 creators for a product launch, negotiate a paid partnership deal, review content drafts for brand compliance, and report creator campaign metrics to the CMO.
suitedFor: People who understand the creator economy
Those who blend business with pop culture awareness
notSuitedFor: People who dislike negotiation
Those who prefer purely technical work
beginnerProjects: Run a micro-influencer campaign for ₹10,000
Build a creator outreach database
Negotiate a free product collab with a local creator

title: Medical Coding Specialist
description: Medical Coding Specialists translate medical procedures, diagnoses, and treatments into standardized codes (ICD-10, CPT, HCPCS) used for billing, insurance claims, and health data analytics.
dayInLife: You review patient records, assign diagnosis and procedure codes, ensure coding accuracy for compliance, query physicians for clarification, and submit billing codes.
suitedFor: People interested in healthcare without clinical roles
Those who enjoy rule-based problem solving
notSuitedFor: People who dislike repetitive pattern matching
Those who need high variability in daily tasks
requiredEducation: Healthcare, Life Sciences, or any field with coding certification
beginnerProjects: Practice coding 50 case scenarios
Complete ICD-10 coding exercises
Pass a mock CPC exam

title: Clinical Research Coordinator
description: Clinical Research Coordinators manage the day-to-day operations of clinical trials, ensuring protocol compliance, participant safety, and accurate data collection.
dayInLife: You screen and enroll participants in a Phase II trial, collect and enter data, coordinate with investigators and sponsors, and prepare for an upcoming regulatory audit.
suitedFor: Life science graduates who enjoy research
People motivated by contributing to medical breakthroughs
notSuitedFor: Those who want direct patient clinical work
People who dislike strict protocol adherence
requiredEducation: Bachelor's or Master's in Life Sciences, Pharmacy, Nursing, or Medicine
beginnerProjects: Study a published clinical trial protocol
Complete CITI GCP training
Shadow a clinical research team

title: Bioinformatics Analyst
description: Bioinformatics Analysts use computational methods to analyze biological data — DNA sequences, protein structures, gene expression — to accelerate drug discovery and biological understanding.
dayInLife: You run a genome alignment pipeline, analyze RNA-seq data to identify differentially expressed genes, visualize findings in R, and present results to a cross-functional research team.
suitedFor: Problem solvers at the biology-technology intersection
notSuitedFor: People who dislike deep technical work
Those who need fast career progress
requiredEducation: Bachelor's or Master's in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Biotechnology, or CS
beginnerProjects: Analyze public genomics data from NCBI
Run a sequence alignment pipeline
Visualize gene expression data in R

title: Food Technologist
description: Food Technologists develop new food products, improve existing ones, ensure food safety, and manage quality standards in the food and beverage industry.
dayInLife: You formulate a new snack product, run sensory evaluations with a panel, review quality control test results, ensure regulatory compliance, and brief the production team.
notSuitedFor: People who prefer desk-only roles
Those who dislike food production environments
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Food Technology, Food Science, or Biotechnology
beginnerProjects: Develop and test a new snack recipe with nutritional labeling
Conduct a shelf-life study
Write a HACCP plan

title: Nutrition Consultant
description: Nutrition Consultants advise individuals, families, and organizations on food choices, dietary plans, and wellness strategies. They may work in clinical settings, corporate wellness, or as independent coaches.
dayInLife: You conduct a dietary assessment with a client, create a personalized meal plan, review a corporate cafeteria menu, and post educational content on your Instagram.
suitedFor: People who love combining science with lifestyle
Entrepreneurs who want to build their own practice
notSuitedFor: Those who prefer clinical medical roles
People who need large organizational structures
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Nutrition, Dietetics, or Food Science
RD registration is required for clinical roles
beginnerProjects: Create 5 personalized meal plans for friends
Start an Instagram nutrition education page
Write a nutrition guide for a specific health goal

title: Drone Operations Specialist
description: Drone Operations Specialists plan, operate, and analyze drone-based missions for industries including agriculture, infrastructure inspection, filmmaking, logistics, and disaster management.
dayInLife: You plan a flight path for an agricultural survey, operate the drone on site, process aerial imagery in software, write an operations report, and ensure regulatory compliance.
suitedFor: Technology enthusiasts who enjoy hands-on work
People who love both outdoor work and data analysis
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike outdoor work
People who are risk-averse about physical operations
beginnerProjects: Get DGCA Remote Pilot License
Complete a survey mission and process data
Build a drone inspection portfolio

title: Event Manager
description: Event Managers plan and execute conferences, corporate events, weddings, concerts, and brand activations. They manage logistics, vendors, budgets, and teams to deliver unforgettable experiences.
dayInLife: You finalize a venue contract, coordinate with 8 vendors, manage the registration portal, supervise setup on event day, and conduct a post-event debrief.
suitedFor: Energetic coordinators who thrive under pressure
Creative problem solvers who love people
notSuitedFor: Those who dislike high-stress deadlines
People who prefer slow-paced environments
requiredEducation: Event Management, Hospitality, or Business preferred
beginnerProjects: Plan and execute a college event end-to-end
Create a detailed event run-of-show document
Negotiate with 3 vendors for a mock event

title: Hotel Management Professional
description: Hotel Management Professionals oversee the operations, guest experience, staff, and financial performance of hotels and hospitality businesses. From front desk to general management, it's a people-first industry.
dayInLife: You review guest feedback scores, handle a VIP guest complaint, lead a staff briefing, review occupancy and revenue per room, and inspect property maintenance standards.
suitedFor: Those who enjoy hospitality and creating experiences
Organized managers who like fast-paced environments
notSuitedFor: Introverts who dislike constant customer interaction
People who need weekends off
requiredEducation: Diploma or Bachelor's in Hotel Management
beginnerProjects: Complete a hotel management internship
Run a mock F&B costing exercise
Write a guest experience improvement plan

title: Translator / Language Specialist
description: Translators convert written content between languages while preserving meaning, tone, and cultural nuance. They work across legal, medical, literary, media, and technical domains.
dayInLife: You translate a 5,000-word legal document from English to Hindi, work on subtitles for a streaming show, review a medical document for terminological accuracy, and quote on a new project.
suitedFor: Independent workers who enjoy intellectual challenges
notSuitedFor: Those who only speak one language
People who dislike isolated desk work
requiredEducation: Bachelor's in Languages, Translation, Linguistics, or any field with language proficiency
preferredBackgrounds: Any field with strong second language
entryPathways: Freelance translation on ProZ or Upwork
beginnerProjects: Translate 10 articles in your language pair
Localize a software UI to your native language
Subtitle a 10-minute video

title: AI Product Tester / AI QA Analyst
description: AI Product Testers evaluate AI systems for accuracy, bias, safety, and reliability. They design test scenarios, identify failure modes, and help build more trustworthy AI products.
dayInLife: You design adversarial prompts to probe a chatbot's safety guardrails, document edge cases, run regression tests on model updates, and brief the product team on risk findings.
suitedFor: Analytical thinkers who enjoy breaking systems
People interested in AI safety and ethics
QA professionals who want to specialize in AI
notSuitedFor: People who want to build AI models
Those who dislike systematic test documentation
requiredEducation: CS, Linguistics, Psychology, or any field with systematic thinking
entryPathways: QA engineer who pivots to AI
beginnerProjects: Red-team a public AI chatbot and document findings
Create a test case library for an LLM use case
Write a bias evaluation report for a model
